title: pseudognaphalium albescens
intro: Pseudognaphalium albescens, also known as white everlasting or silver everlasting, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its silvery-gray foliage and white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have medicinal properties in reducing pain, swelling, and infection. The leaves and flowers of Pseudognaphalium albescens can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves to promote health benefits including wound healing, skin protection, and respiratory relief.
